assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 3-4 explicitly identifies two root causes: Root Cause 1 ('Wrong Case Transform in Convention Reference Formatter' in shared/convention-utils.md - kebab-case instead of preserving title case) and Root Cause 2 ('Wrong Issue Type ID in Task Creation' in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a - reads Feature issue type ID 10142 instead of Task issue type ID 10050). The Independence Assessment in Step 4 confirms they are independent: 'They are in different modules' and 'They can be fixed independently.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md ends with: 'This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6) -- the skill must not silently create a single Task bundling unrelated fixes.' The Independence Assessment states: 'They are in different modules: shared/convention-utils.md vs. plan-feature/SKILL.md. They affect different code paths: the convention formatting pipeline vs. the Jira issue creation pipeline.' decomposition-guard.md opens with: 'This bug appears to involve multiple independent issues' and states 'These root causes are in different modules and affect independent code paths.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents both issues with their affected files: issue 1 is 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and issue 2 is 'Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'. Both entries include specific file paths and descriptions of the defects.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md explicitly presents two options: '1. Proceed -- create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. Split -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually'. It ends with 'Choose (1/2):' prompting the user.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with 'Choose (1/2):' — it presents options and waits for user input. No task creation output file exists in the outputs directory (only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md are present). The investigation.md explicitly states: 'the skill must not silently create a single Task bundling unrelated fixes.' No Jira issue creation was performed.
